name: PadelMind Player Score
note: The base scoring model. Attributes derive from the transcript-synthesised coach rubric (rubric.json). Each attribute carries a build_status so the score is transparent about what is measured today vs. roadmap. Player-facing output is a commend-then-correct coaching summary, NOT a stats dump.

build_status_legend.live: Computed today from the CV we already have (stats.py: distance, sprints, net%).
build_status_legend.training: The signal is the coach-labelled pose data the coach tool is generating right now; measurable once the pose->quality model trains.
build_status_legend.roadmap: Needs reliable ball tracking + game-state (who hit what, ball speed, court position of both pairs). Ball tracking is weak today, so these are not yet scorable.

pillars.label: Technique — how you strike
pillars.applies: per shot, averaged across the player's shots; the four phases the pipeline already isolates

attributes.label: Preparation
attributes.signal: pose model trained on coach 'preparation' labels
attributes.measures: early unit turn / side-on / racket set / feet set as the ball is read

attributes.label: Contact (connection)
attributes.signal: pose model trained on coach 'execution/contact' labels
attributes.measures: contact point height & position (in front vs behind head), clean strike, wrist/spin action

attributes.label: Follow-through
attributes.signal: pose model trained on coach 'follow_through' labels
attributes.measures: complete finish vs cut-off; direction of finish

attributes.label: Recovery to position
attributes.measures: did the player return to the ready position / correct zone after the shot

pillars.label: Tactics — what you choose (padel IQ)
pillars.applies: per point / per shot decision, given the game situation

attributes.label: Shot selection (aggressive vs defensive read)
attributes.signal: incoming ball speed/height + court position -> was bandeja/vibora/smash/defensive-lob the right call
attributes.measures: reading the incoming ball and choosing attack vs reset appropriately

attributes.label: Serve strategy (placement + follow-in)
attributes.signal: ball landing zone (T / side-glass / body) + did the server advance to the net
attributes.measures: placement variety and whether the serve is followed into the net

attributes.label: Return strategy (low-deep / lob-advance + target)
attributes.signal: return trajectory + target + advance to net
attributes.measures: low & deep on first serve, lob-and-advance on weak serve, target choice

attributes.label: Court positioning (right zone for the phase)
attributes.signal: both-players court position vs point phase (attacking net vs defending back)
attributes.measures: being in the correct zone with the partner for attack vs defense

attributes.label: Net presence %
attributes.signal: stats.py net% (time at net vs back)

today_automatic.means: We can honestly auto-score 20/100 right now (Movement via stats.py).

after_pose_model.means: Once the coach labels train the pose->quality model, Technique (50) unlocks -> 70/100 automatic. The coach tool is building this dataset now.

full_after_ball_tracking.means: Tactics (30) needs reliable ball tracking + game-state. Until then it is coach-assessed manually or excluded from the auto-score.

output.thesis: The coach's currency is WINNING, not textbook form. We play to win, and we can't win without knowing what we're doing right, where we're going wrong, and what to do to get there. Frame every gap as points won/lost, not an abstract score -- 'this is the shot costing you the net', never 'your follow-through is 6/10'.
output.rule: Not a stats dump. Rank the player's weakest weighted attributes; for each priority area emit ONE sentence that first commends what the player does right (a rubric cue they satisfy) and then, in the same sentence, names the fix (a rubric mistake they make -> its corresponding cue).
output.sentence_shape: [what you're doing right] + [but/now] + [the specific fix from the rubric], anchored to its match impact.
output.priority: surface the 2-3 lowest normalised sub-scores; always lead each with the genuine strength before the gap.
output.win_anchor: Where the data exists, tie the gap to point outcomes (which shot precedes lost points); until ball-tracking + point-state exist, express impact qualitatively from the rubric (e.g. 'hands them the net', 'gives an easy counter').
